calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Amended Summons And Second Amended Complaint On Defendant Mohammed Daqa
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GROVE STREET MARKET's Motion To Quash Service Of Amended Summons And Second Amended Complaint On Defendant Mohammed Daqa.

Defendant Mohammed Daqa's motion to quash service of amended summons and second amended complaint is granted. Service of summons may be quashed if service was improper and the Court therefore lacks jurisdiction of the defendant. (Code Civ. Proc. 418.10; Fuss v. City of Los Angeles (1958) 162 Cal.App.2d 643, 646-647.) Although the Proof of Service submitted as Exhibit B to the Declaration of Ryan Whyte shows that the registered process server declares under penalty of perjury that he personally served Mr. Daqa on March 25, 2019 at 1:12 p.m., this merely creates a rebuttable presumption of proper service. (See Evid. Code 647 ["The return of a process server registered pursuant to Chapter 16 (commencing with Section 22350) of Division 8 of the Business and Professions Code upon process or notice establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence, of the facts stated in the return."].) This presumption may be rebutted by evidence that contradicts it. (City of Los Angeles v. Morgan (1951) 105 Cal.App.2d 726, 731.) The Declaration of Said Abdelmannan does just that by stating under penalty of perjury that, at the time of attempted service, Mr. Daqa was not present and was no longer employed by Defendant Grove Street Market. (Abdelmannan Decl. 5, 7.) Furthermore, Mr. Abdelmannan said that he had not seen Mr. Daqa for several months and believed he may be out of the country. (Id. 5.) Plaintiff Mitra Satram's one-page opposition does nothing to call into question Mr. Abdelmannan's credibility or the veracity of his statements.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
